domain: Bristlemouth eye-reference photophore counterillumination (ventral photophores + anterior-pigmented eye-facing photophore; brain closes the loop on (downwelling, own emission) so silhouette to predator is cancelled in real time)
specific_mechanism: Real-time closed-loop active camouflage where the agent SIMULTANEOUSLY samples its own emission and the ambient downwelling reference, then nullifies the difference at the receiver-facing channel.
llm_application: ECED (Eye-reference Counter-Emission Decoder): an LLM decoder running an auxiliary live-sample of ambient human-text distribution and modulating its own token sampling (temperature, top-p, rare-token injection) to minimize KL between its running output statistics and the ambient reference at each step — eliminating its statistical signature to any AI-text detector via continuous live-reference cancellation.
novelty_claim: Adaptive Text Watermark (2401.13927) selects high-entropy tokens; RLCracker (2509.20924) adversarially trains an attacker post-hoc; linguistic-aware watermark (2510.13829) uses syntactic predictability. None close the loop in real time on a live-sampled ambient distribution to drive ambient-vs-emission KL → 0 the way ECED does (analogous to bristlemouth's eye-facing photophore continuously referencing downwelling light).
